channel.title: Morning Education
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Education examines the latest news in education politics and policy.

item.title: The looming education funding gap Congress will face
item.description: The Education Department just took a big step toward fulfilling Donald Trump’s pledge to boost workforce programs that don’t require a four-year degree. Now Congress has to figure out how to pay for a looming funding shortfall.
item.dc:creator: By Mackenzie Wilkes

item.title: ‘A sinking ship’: Employees flee ED’s civil rights office
item.description: The Education Department’s Office for Civil Rights has been President Donald Trump’s greatest weapon in forcing schools to bend to his will. But the office is hemorrhaging staff.
item.dc:creator: By Bianca Quilantan and Hannah Psalma Ramirez
item.dc:contributor: By Bianca Quilantan and Hannah Psalma Ramirez

item.title: The agency Trump wants to close is hiring
item.description: Top leaders at the Education Department have made it known they feel their agency is “unnecessary.” But that’s not keeping them from hiring a lot more people.
item.dc:creator: By Rebecca Carballo

item.title: Why Congress is struggling with Trump's education agency breakup
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to move career and technical education programs to the Labor Department. Lawmaker resistance could be a big deal.
item.dc:creator: By Mackenzie Wilkes

item.title: The accreditation overhaul issue that could boil over
item.description: The Education Department’s wide-ranging accreditation proposal wants to address a long-standing higher education issue: transferring credits between colleges.
item.dc:creator: By Bianca Quilantan

item.title: Debate rages over the cost of educating immigrant students
item.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to expel unauthorized immigrants from the United States is trickling into the nation’s classrooms and college campuses.
item.dc:creator: By Mackenzie Wilkes

item.title: Why college oversight could get more political
item.description: The Trump administration is forging ahead in its quest to rid higher education of “radical left” oversight organizations.
item.dc:creator: By Rebecca Carballo

item.title: The coming blue-state battles over trans athletes
item.description: The movement to bar transgender students from competing on women and girls’ sports teams is heating up in unexpected battlegrounds.
item.dc:creator: By Bianca Quilantan
